---
title: "Creating & Using Skills"
---

::: {.topic-badges}
[Moderate]{.level-badge .level-moderate} · **Technical & Agentic** track · ~1 hour, self-paced
:::

[← Back to the Technical & Agentic track](technical-agentic.qmd){.track-back}

*Turn a prompt that works into a reusable, shareable skill your team can run again and again.*

## What you'll be able to do

- Write a reusable skill: job, steps, output, and guardrails
- Reuse it across tasks and share it with colleagues
- Know when a skill beats a one-off prompt

## Overview

A skill is a saved package of instructions for one job done well — "turn notes into our standard recap." Write it once; the assistant applies it whenever that job comes up. Claude has a formal Skills feature; in ChatGPT Edu, custom GPTs and project instructions do the same.

Most quality lives in the instructions, not the model. A good skill names the job, the steps, the output format, and the guardrails.

## Practice activities

```{=html}
<details class="activity"><summary>Activity 1 · Moderate — Write a skill</summary>
```

1. Pick a repeated task (a recap, a rewrite, a checklist).
2. Write a skill with **job, steps, output format, and guardrails** (no sensitive data).
3. Test it on two different inputs; tighten where it drifts.

```{=html}
</details>
```

```{=html}
<details class="activity"><summary>Activity 2 · Moderate — Share and reuse</summary>
```

1. Save the skill as a custom GPT or shared doc your team can reach.
2. Have a colleague run it and note where the instructions need to be clearer.

```{=html}
</details>
```

## Check your readiness

Answer these, then check — your score suggests whether to dive in or skim the readings first.

<div class="readiness-quiz">
<script type="application/json" class="quiz-data">
{"questions": [{"q": "Most of a skill's quality comes from…", "options": ["Clear instructions", "A bigger model", "More examples", "Longer prompts"], "answer": 0, "explain": "Instructions carry the quality."}, {"q": "A skill is worth writing when a task is…", "options": ["Repeated and worth standardizing", "One-off", "Trivial", "Secret"], "answer": 0, "explain": "Reuse is the payoff."}, {"q": "A good skill includes…", "options": ["Job, steps, output, and guardrails", "Just a title", "Only an example", "A password"], "answer": 0, "explain": "Those four parts make it reliable and shareable."}]}
</script>
</div>

## Recommended readings

From the AI readings library (ask the facilitator for access):

- **The Complete Guide to Building Skills for Claude** — how to structure a reusable skill.
- **AI Agent Skills: Why Skill Curation Is the Next Bottleneck** — keeping a useful library of skills.

## Useful resources

- [Claude Skills (docs)](https://docs.claude.com) — Claude's formal Skills feature.
- [ChatGPT Edu (Drexel AI Tools)](https://drexel.edu/provost/ai/tools) — the approved tool, with data rules.

::: {.callout-warning}
## Data rules
Keep sensitive data out of skill instructions and example files; share **inside Drexel** only. See [Tools & Access](../tools.qmd) and Drexel's [AI Tools page](https://drexel.edu/provost/ai/tools).
:::
